retry_reason: put operations always execute; failure is not expected (kref_put handles underflow automatically via BUG). Retrying a put would be a double-put bug.
contract_summary: Release wrapper for an MTD device reference. Decrements the kref refcount of the given mtd and all its ancestors (partitions up to the master), then releases the module reference and calls the master's _put_device callback. After this call, the caller must not use the mtd object. This is the final put path; it is safe to call multiple times only if the refcount was previously incremented correctly. No return value; no error handling needed. The function integrates partition hierarchy traversal and final device cleanup.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement refcounts (kref_put) on an MTD device and its parents, then release module reference and device. The loop and additional calls (module_put, _put_device) are essential parts of the release sequence, not independent business logic.

check.reason: kref_put(&mtd->refcnt, mtd_device_release) in the loop matches the operation: put on the first parameter's refcnt.refcount. The access path mtd.refcnt.refcount and type chain mtd_info--kref--refcount_t are consistent with the code (mtd->refcnt is struct kref, which contains refcount_t). The segment count (3) matches the number of types (3).
